schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Practical Suggestions You Can Use Immediately: ω_m

theory.canonical_statement: ### Practical suggestions you can use immediately - If you want to **target 6-fold patterns**, try κ ≈ 2π as a simple first test (it gives a strong \(J_6\)). In simulation or lab: set phase modulation depth so the effective κ ≈ 2π, then measure whether 6-fold nodal geometry dominates creation/aggregation. - If you want to **avoid resonance** and keep creation diffuse, choose carrier/modulation frequencies so their ratios are irrational multiples (use π explicitly), e.g. set \(\omega_m = \pi \times f_0\) or choose modulation index κ = π × (an irrational factor). - If you want detailed numbers: I can scan κ more finely to find the *global* maxima of \(|J_6(κ)|\) (the exact “magic κ” values beyond 2π), and return the top few peaks and their κ locations so you can tune experiments precisely.
theory.technical_interpretation: Practical Suggestions You Can Use Immediately: ω_m is an algebraic definition, written ω_m = π × f_0. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from π, f_0, defining ω_m. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where if you want to avoid resonance and keep creation diffuse, choose carrier/modulation frequencies so their ratios are irrational…
